design_philosophy.core_loop: Progress through linear stages defeating waves of enemies using melee combat and limited special moves
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Managing health and positioning against multiple simultaneous enemies in confined spaces
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Urban brawler taking down street gangs with martial arts and environmental hazards
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Classic beat-em-up philosophy prioritizing immediate combat feedback and repetitive skill execution over narrative complexity; emphasizes co-op dynamics where player coordination is essential

difficulty.lives_system: Limited lives with game over state; continue system available
difficulty.continues_system: Continue mechanic present, allowing progression past failure without full restart
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Minimal; difficulty is fixed. Co-op play affects effective difficulty through support mechanics

progression.unlock_mechanism: Sequential stage progression; no branching paths
replayability_hooks: 1-player vs 2-player mode differences in enemy patterns and ally AI
Speedrun optimization potential (3m 41s WR suggests tight routing)
Deathless run challenge (one entry shows successful no-death completion)

world_building_techniques: Urban setting implied through stage design
Enemy archetypes establish antagonist faction visually

faction_indicators: Street gang enemies vs player protagonist
danger_signals: Crowd control scenarios (multiple enemies per stage)
Health depletion as primary failure state
environmental_storytelling: Urban locations establish turf warfare context
Stage-to-stage progression implies territory reclamation narrative

sources_used: speedrun.com - Double Dragon (Genesis) leaderboards and game metadata
